summary.intent: Shrink a reasoning combo button/dropdown to fit its content rather than stretching to a fixed wide width.
summary.outcome: Fixed the dropdown width by overriding a hardcoded `width: 280px` on `.model-dropdown` in input.css for the reasoning combo context.

repo: The model dropdown has a hardcoded `width: 280px` in crates/web/ui/input.css that overrides any flex/auto sizing on the parent button.

code.finding: .model-dropdown has hardcoded width: 280px; reasoning combo required a scoped override to shrink it.

workflow: CSS width fixes on dropdowns require checking both the trigger button and the dropdown element itself — fixing the button alone left the dropdown visually wide.

friction: First fix (width: auto with justify-content: flex-start on the button) appeared to solve the problem but the dropdown's own hardcoded width still made it look too wide, requiring a second investigation.
